A new leak also suggests an upgraded dual rear camera unit, including two 48-megapixel sensors. The iPhone Air 2 is expected to go official next year alongside the standard iPhone 18. ...

Its advanced capabilities, including agentic threat investigation and an AI assistant, aim to provide rapid, automated responses to sophisticated cyberattacks, a growing concern in India.

articles.title: Delhi roads are about to go digital: 2,500 QR codes planned so residents can check road history & report damage in seconds
articles.description: Delhi's Public Works Department is launching 'Know Your Road', a project enabling citizens to report road issues via smartphone scans. Nearly 2,500 QR codes will be installed on PWD-managed roads within 60 days, providing access to road maintenance history, contractor details, and complaint tracking. This initiative aims to boost transparency and accountability in road maintenance, empowering residents to actively participate in infrastructure monitoring.

articles.title: Tata Tigor facelift patent images out! What to expect, changes explained
articles.description: Tata Motors is gearing up to launch a refreshed Tigor sedan, following the Tiago's update. Patent images reveal a sharper exterior with redesigned lights and bumpers. Inside, expect a modern cabin mirroring the Tiago, boasting a larger touchscreen, digital cluster, and new steering wheel. The familiar petrol and CNG powertrains are expected to continue, promising a competitive offering in the sub-four-metre segment.

articles.title: ET Prime Special Series: Multibagger or IBC - Part 59: Japan taught it. Tata bought it. Now EVs will test this auto ancillary
articles.description: This is a story about a company that is simultaneously an asset and an anomaly. An asset because it builds a tangible, necessary product, generates surplus cash, and has demonstrated longevity across economic cycles. An anomaly because its entire commercial existence is structured around one buyer, who also happens to be its promoter. The company has adapted through multiple transitions, from diesel to CNG and now to EV. But the deeper question is whether the relationship with its dominant promoter-customer is a moat or a ceiling.

articles.title: This Man's Weekend Digital Detox Exposed A Hard Truth About Modern Life

articles.title: Wordle Answer Today (June 22): Puzzle 1829 Too Hard For You? Check Hints, & Solution
articles.description:
Wordle Answer Today: Wordle players were greeted this Monday, June 22, with another fun puzzle that got many people thinking. The popular five-letter word game continues to challenge players every day with a new word to guess in just six tries. Some people enjoy solving it as fast as they can, while others are focused on keeping their winning streak alive.

Today’s puzzle was a little tricky, but the hints gave players a fair chance to figure it out. If you’re still searching for the answer, don’t worry, we’ve got both the solution and its meaning below.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle may seem easy, but it takes careful thinking and smart guesses. Players have six chances to find the hidden five-letter word. After each guess, the tiles change colour to show helpful clues:

  • Green tiles: correct letter in the correct place.
  • Yellow tiles: correct letter, but in the wrong place.
  • Grey tiles: the letter is not in the word.

By paying attention to these clues, players can rule out wrong letters and get closer to the correct answer. This simple but clever gameplay is what keeps millions of people coming back every day.

Hints That Helped Crack The June 22 Puzzle

Today's clues gave players a good chance to work out the answer:

  • Like an egg.
  • The word begins with O.
  • It ends with E.
  • The word has 3 vowels.
  • All five letters are unique.
  • Using "venom" as a starter reveals three yellow letters.

These hints pointed players in the right direction without giving away the answer too early.

Wordle Answer Today (June 22)

The answer to today's Wordle is: OVATE.

The word "ovate" means something that has an oval or egg-like shape. It is often used to describe eggs, leaves, or other objects that are wider in the middle and narrower at the ends.

Congratulations if you guessed today's word correctly! If not, there's always another Wordle waiting tomorrow.

articles.title: 'We don’t think...': BioCompute founder says India is not ready for DNA data storage, moves to San Francisco

articles.description: As AI agents become more capable, some developers believe the era of carefully crafted prompts is giving way to something more powerful. A growing number of engineers are building automated loops that can direct AI systems, assign work and manage tasks with little human involvement.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg quote of the day: “The biggest risk is not taking any risk”; Meta CEO's powerful reminder on why avoiding risks may be the biggest obstacle to success
articles.description: Mark Zuckerberg's assertion, 'The biggest risk is not taking any risk,' resonates deeply in our evolving world. Avoiding risks, while seemingly safe, can actually be more perilous than embracing them. Stepping beyond comfort zones, whether in career, education, or personal pursuits, is crucial for growth and unlocking potential. While not every risk guarantees success, the experience gained is invaluable, making inaction the true danger.

articles.title: DoT drafts spectrum rules for satellite, BSNL; NGSO out
articles.description: New draft rules from the Department of Telecommunications will allow administrative spectrum assignment for state-run firms like BSNL and MTNL, law enforcement, and private satellite/broadcasting companies. These rules specifically apply to geostationary orbit (GSO) satellite users, including VSAT and teleport operators, with spectrum fees set at 3-4% of adjusted gross revenue. Non-GSO players like Starlink are awaiting separate pricing and approval processes.

articles.title: Apple Vision Pro vs Snap Specs: Two visions of face-worn computing, compared
articles.description: Apple wants you to step into a virtual world, while Snap wants you to stay in the real one. Here's how their very different approaches to spatial computing compare. Apple Vision Pro [left] vs Snap Specs [right] The launch of Snap Specs at Augmented World Expo on June 16 is a big shift forward for the social company. After the previous effort of Snap Spectacles, Snap Specs are a step closer to the augmented reality future by being smart glasses with a built-in display. This is something that brings Snap's efforts in line with the Ray-Ban eyewear that Meta has produced, including its yet-to-ship Meta Ray-Ban Display. It's also a massively different product from Apple's own head-mounted computing device, the Apple Vision Pro . Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: Delhivery launches AI-native mapping suite “Delhivery Maps”
articles.description: Delhivery has unveiled Delhivery Maps, India's first AI-driven mapping suite for commercial logistics, marking its 15th anniversary and entry into geospatial infrastructure. This innovative platform, powered by its proprietary Naksha LLM and extensive shipment data, addresses unique challenges in commercial shipping, including unstructured addresses and vehicle-specific routing. The company now utilizes this suite for its entire nationwide network, offering APIs for developers to integrate advanced location intelligence into their applications.

articles.title: Tiny Ann Arbor Apple Store will move to a bigger space in July
articles.description: The Apple Store at Briarwood Mall in Ann Arbor, Michigan, is getting an upgrade. It's set to shift over to a larger space in the same mall at the end of July. Apple Briarwood - Image Credit: Apple The Apple Briarwood store is one of the smallest outlets in Apple's retail empire. However, that will soon change for the better. In Sunday's "Power On" newsletter for Bloomberg , Mark Gurman writes that the Briarwood Mall store is getting a major upgrade. It will be changing its spot to a larger space within the same shopping center. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums
